scope_contract: In --changed-files mode this inspector emits findings for the listed files and tags each finding with introduced_in_diff: bool per Mechanism (ii). Findings with introduced_in_diff: false are advisory/non-blocking per the default-safe rule (AC-2.6(b)).

obsolete_functionality.reason: Function _is_overnight_active() is documented as dead code in the module's own revision history (line 72): 'retained as dead code for reference but is no longer consulted by main()'. It is defined but never called anywhere in the codebase. This is pre-existing, not introduced in this diff (present in b58b4bc before the 86608dc change). No cleanup action is recommended per the file's explicit retention intent.
obsolete_functionality.recommendation: Documented intentional dead-code reference; no cleanup action recommended unless policy changes.

changes_introduced.description: Added match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command to import from lib.allowlist (line 92). The import is used at line 157 — not an unreferenced import.

_check_git_allowlist(): removed early-return subagent firewall, added sentinel grant check (NOT gated by IS_SUBAGENT), restored legacy subagent firewall for the legacy path only. Per task 20260524-133650 / M2 decision (20260521-090200).

summary.close_gate_rationale: The single finding has introduced_in_diff: false. Per the default-safe rule (AC-2.6(b)), file-level findings without an explicit positive new-in-this-diff marker are treated as pre-existing/advisory/non-blocking. Zero findings are newly introduced in this diff.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed: (1) Finding 1 is correctly tagged as pre-existing (not introduced in diff). (2) Severity 'major' was overstated for documented intentional dead-code retention — PROPOSED_FIX to downgrade to minor/advisory. (3) No additional cleanliness issues missed in the introduced diff. (4) Safety Rules compliance favors not recommending deletion of _is_overnight_active() — PROPOSED_FIX to phrase as 'no cleanup action recommended unless policy changes'.

findings.issue: Finding 1 correctly tagged as pre-existing, not introduced in diff. Evidence: _is_overnight_active() and the module docstring note existed in b58b4bc before the 86608dc diff.
findings.rationale: Confirms correct pre-existing classification. No change needed.

findings.issue: Severity 'major' overstated for documented intentional dead-code retention. The file explicitly says the helper is retained 'for reference' and no longer consulted, so this is not an accidental cleanup defect.
findings.proposed_fix: Downgrade to minor/advisory, with introduced_in_diff: false and blocking: false.
findings.rationale: The finding was already tagged introduced_in_diff: false (non-blocking), but severity has been corrected to minor to match the intentional-retention context. A major severity implies actionable cleanup urgency which is not warranted here.

findings.issue: No additional cleanliness issues were missed in the introduced diff. The added import is used at line 157, changed logic/comments are task-rationale, not temp files, duplicates, misplaced docs, archive candidates, or orphaned code.
findings.rationale: Validates completeness of the diff scan.

findings.issue: Safety Rules compliance favors not recommending deletion of _is_overnight_active() as an automatic cleanup. It is intentionally retained per the module-level revision history.
findings.proposed_fix: If retained in report, phrase as: 'Documented intentional dead-code reference; no cleanup action recommended unless policy changes.'
findings.rationale: Wording updated in the finding to match the proposed phrasing. Deletion is not recommended.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied two PROPOSED_FIX items: (1) severity downgraded from major to minor for the _is_overnight_active() dead-code finding; (2) recommendation wording updated to 'Documented intentional dead-code reference; no cleanup action recommended unless policy changes.'
